content:
- Completed task 10 end-to-end: added new `src/test_harness` module family (`namespace`, `ports`, `pg16`, `etcd3`, `tls`, `auth`) and wired it under `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-harness"))]` in `src/lib.rs` with Cargo feature gate + tokio `process/net` features.
- Implemented namespace lifecycle (unique IDs + temp dirs + idempotent cleanup + RAII guard), port reservation allocator with ownership-based release semantics, PG/etcd data-dir prep helpers, async PG16/etcd3 spawners with namespace-local logging, TCP readiness polling, and graceful-first shutdown with forced-kill fallback.
- Added harness tests for uniqueness/idempotent cleanup/concurrent reservations and binary-gated spawn smoke tests; caught and fixed a false-collision test bug by retaining reservations during uniqueness assertions.
- Verification evidence: `cargo test test_harness -- --nocapture` passed; then full gates passed in sequence: `make check`, `make test`, `make test-bdd`, `make lint`.
- Updated task file with skeptical-review amendments, switched to NOW EXECUTE, completed checklist/status tags (`<status>done</status> <passes>true</passes> <passing>true</passing>`), ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and recorded a new cross-task learning in `AGENTS.md` about keeping port reservations alive in collision tests.
- Next immediate action after this log: stage all modified files including `.ralph` bookkeeping files and commit with required `task finished ...` message.

- Read progress log and parsed .ralph/current_tasks.md.
- No <passes>meta-task</passes> entries found. Multiple tasks have <passes>false</passes>.
- Applied priority + story-chain logic: selected .ralph/tasks/story-rust-system-harness/04-task-pginfo-worker-single-query-and-real-pg-tests.md because it is high priority and a direct prerequisite for task 08, which is required to unblock ultra_high task 12 chain.
- Wrote selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stopped.

- Loaded memory with `.ralph/progress_read.sh`, read `.ralph/current_task.txt`, and opened task 04 (`04-task-pginfo-worker-single-query-and-real-pg-tests.md`).
- Per user flow, I performed reconnaissance only (no execution): inspected all current `src/pginfo/*` stubs, worker contracts, state primitives, Make targets, and test harness modules in parallel command batches to reduce context uncertainty.
- Wrote a full execution plan into task 04 under `<execution_plan>` with explicit implementation/test/verification/bookkeeping steps; appended `TO BE VERIFIED` and intentionally stopped before any code execution.
- Key planning notes: one-query poll contract, strict no-unwrap error handling, WAL/timeline parsing strategy, real PG16 integration expectations with binary-gated skipping, and required make-gate sequence.
- Next step for the next turn: detect `TO BE VERIFIED`, run deep skeptical review, alter at least one plan item, then replace trailer with `NOW EXECUTE`.
